heading: Possessing or selling impressions of tools used for obligations or securities
label: 477 Possessing or selling impressions of tools used for obligations or securities
text: Whoever, with intent to defraud, possesses, keeps, safeguards, or controls, without authority from the United States, any imprint, stamp, analog, digital, or electronic image, or impression, taken or made upon any substance or material whatsoever, of any tool, implement, instrument or thing, used, fitted or intended to be used, for any of the purposes mentioned in ; orsection 476 of this titleWhoever, with intent to defraud, sells, gives, or delivers any such imprint, stamp, analog, digital, or electronic image, or impression to any other person—Shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 25 years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 267 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 15335 Stat. 1117Changes in phraseology were made.

Amendments2001— inserted “analog, digital, or electronic image,” after “imprint, stamp,” in first and second pars. and substituted “25 years” for “ten years” in third par.Pub. L. 107–561994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $5,000”.Pub. L. 103–322
